source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Add and subtract numbers using concrete objects, pictorial representations, and mentally, including a two-digit number and ones, a two-digit number and tens, and two two-digit numbers.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches place-value based addition and subtraction with base-ten blocks before mental strategy selection.

objective.child_goal: I can add and subtract two-digit numbers using tens and ones.
objective.statement: Use place value, base-ten models and mental strategies to add and subtract two-digit numbers.
misconceptions: Adds all digits as separate ones without preserving tens and ones.
Ignores regrouping when ones cross ten.
Chooses addition when a subtraction comparison is needed.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Groups of ten in a number.
vocabulary.audio_script: The tens tell us how many full groups of ten we have.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Single pieces left after the tens.
vocabulary.audio_script: The ones are the single cubes.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Swap ten ones for one ten, or one ten for ten ones.
vocabulary.audio_script: When ten ones join together, we can swap them for one ten.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Ten loose cubes sliding into a ten-rod.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check place-value representation before adding and subtracting.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Thirty-four has three tens and four ones.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Base-ten rods and cubes snap into a place-value mat.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The workshop has 34 gears. Add 18 more by building tens and ones.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce addition as combining tens and ones with regrouping when needed.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Add the tens, add the ones, then see whether ten ones can make a new ten.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A workshop bench with rods and cubes.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: 34 plus 18 makes 52 because twelve ones become one ten and two ones.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model regrouping concretely before symbolic calculation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Four ones and eight ones make twelve ones. Ten of them become one new ten.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Twelve cubes group into one rod plus two cubes.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Show that subtracting tens changes the tens while ones stay the same.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Take away two tens. The six ones are still there.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two ten-rods slide off the mat.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check strategy choice for subtracting near ten.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: You might count back nine, or take seven then two more.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A number line shows jumps back from 57.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Nova why ten ones can become one ten.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you show me the swap?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion holds ten loose cubes and asks what to do.

manipulatives.purpose: Let learners construct, combine, exchange and remove tens and ones before selecting mental strategies.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners drag rods and cubes, group ten ones into one rod, and mirror jumps on a number line.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Supports keyboard increment controls, audio labels for tens and ones, and reduced-motion instant swaps.

manipulatives.purpose: Connect two-digit calculation to efficient jumps of tens and ones while keeping direction and landing number visible.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose a starting number, program forward or backward jumps of 10 and 1, then launch a friendly rover and explain the landing number.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Supports keyboard and single-switch jump controls, typed jump sequences and tap buttons; static mode shows numbered arcs, while reduced-motion mode reveals each landing point without rover travel.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave no-regrouping and regrouping tasks only after tens-and-ones language is secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build confidence adding two-digit numbers without regrouping.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two addends + base-ten mat + no ones total above nine
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include two-digit plus ones and two-digit plus tens before two two-digit addends.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Practise exchanging ten ones for one ten in addition.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ones sum from 10-18 + required swap + answer choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Animation must visibly preserve quantity during the swap.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Connect subtraction to removing tens, removing ones and jumping back.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start number + subtract tens or ones + number-line jumps
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors should include addition results for operation diagnosis.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Ask learners to choose efficient strategies after concrete models.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept multiple valid strategies when explanation matches.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply addition and subtraction to short picture-supported contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: workshop story + quantities + build model + select operation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep text short and provide audio narration.

body.prompt: Build 34 + 18. What is the total?
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Add the tens and ones separately.
Ten of the ones can become one new ten.
question_variants.explanation: 34 + 18 = 52 because 4 ones plus 8 ones makes 12 ones, which regroup as 1 ten and 2 ones.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 52. 34 + 18 = 52 because 4 ones plus 8 ones makes 12 ones, which regroup as 1 ten and 2 ones.
feedback.repair: Add the tens and ones separately. Ten of the ones can become one new ten. Use that clue to repair ignores regrouping, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this base ten build, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 52: 34 + 18 = 52 because 4 ones plus 8 ones makes 12 ones, which regroup as 1 ten and 2 ones.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Build 46, then take away 20. What is left?
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

question_variants.explanation: 46 - 20 = 26 because two tens are removed and the six ones stay.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 26. 46 - 20 = 26 because two tens are removed and the six ones stay.
feedback.repair: 20 is two tens. Take away two ten-rods. Use that clue to repair operation choice confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this base ten build, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 26: 46 - 20 = 26 because two tens are removed and the six ones stay.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Use jumps to find 57 - 9.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

question_variants.explanation: 57 - 9 can be solved by 57 - 7 = 50, then 50 - 2 = 48.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 48. 57 - 9 can be solved by 57 - 7 = 50, then 50 - 2 = 48.
feedback.repair: Take away 7 to reach 50. Then take away 2 more. Use that clue to repair subtracting across ten, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this number line, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 48: 57 - 9 can be solved by 57 - 7 = 50, then 50 - 2 = 48.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Which strategy is most efficient for 63 - 20?
choices: Remove 2 tens
Remove 2 ones
Add 2 tens
Count back from 1
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The three ones do not need to change.
question_variants.explanation: Removing two tens from 63 gives 43. The three ones stay the same.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "Remove 2 tens". Removing two tens from 63 gives 43. The three ones stay the same.
feedback.repair: 20 is made from two tens. The three ones do not need to change. Use that clue to repair operation choice confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this tap choice, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "Remove 2 tens": Removing two tens from 63 gives 43. The three ones stay the same.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: One-cube mission 1: build 20, then add 1 ones.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move named blocks or record the child's indicated step without supplying the calculation.
body.concrete_route: Base-ten rods and cubes on labelled tens/ones mats, with automatic exchange and undo available.
body.number_line_route: Static or stepwise labelled jumps of tens and ones; movement animation is optional.
body.part_whole_route: A labelled whole-and-parts bar can replace dense text or block manipulation.
body.visual_route: One low-clutter representation per panel with large numerals, aligned place-value labels and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal build, exchange or jump steps separately; preserve correct work and reduce choices when useful.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed steps can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.retry_message: Your correct blocks and steps stay. Choose another model or clue and continue.

question_variants.explanation: 20 + 1 = 21; only the ones change.

feedback.correct: The calculation and model agree. 20 + 1 = 21; only the ones change.
feedback.repair: Separate tens and ones on a labelled mat, keep correct groups and rebuild only the first place-value step that does not match.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 20 + 1 = 21; only the ones change.
feedback.strategy_message: Building, jumping, partitioning, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid evidence; speed and handwriting are not scored.

body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move named blocks or record the child's indicated step without supplying the calculation.
body.concrete_route: Base-ten rods and cubes on labelled tens/ones mats, with automatic exchange and undo available.
body.number_line_route: Static or stepwise labelled jumps of tens and ones; movement animation is optional.
body.part_whole_route: A labelled whole-and-parts bar can replace dense text or block manipulation.
body.visual_route: One low-clutter representation per panel with large numerals, aligned place-value labels and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal build, exchange or jump steps separately; preserve correct work and reduce choices when useful.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed steps can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: repair a calm invention workshop using one checked calculation
gamification.reward: one blueprint spark for a model, strategy or check
gamification.retry_message: Your correct blocks and steps stay. Choose another model or clue and continue.

question_variants.explanation: 14 + 16 = 30. 10 ones regroup as one new ten and 0 ones without changing quantity.

feedback.correct: The calculation and model agree. 14 + 16 = 30. 10 ones regroup as one new ten and 0 ones without changing quantity.
feedback.repair: Keep every block visible. Exchange exactly ten ones for one ten, confirm the quantity is unchanged, then continue from the first uncertain step.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 14 + 16 = 30. 10 ones regroup as one new ten and 0 ones without changing quantity.
feedback.strategy_message: Building, jumping, partitioning, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid evidence; speed and handwriting are not scored.

Choose and model each operation in story order.
Check with an inverse, estimate or second representation.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move named blocks or record the child's indicated step without supplying the calculation.
body.concrete_route: Base-ten rods and cubes on labelled tens/ones mats, with automatic exchange and undo available.
body.number_line_route: Static or stepwise labelled jumps of tens and ones; movement animation is optional.
body.part_whole_route: A labelled whole-and-parts bar can replace dense text or block manipulation.
body.visual_route: One low-clutter representation per panel with large numerals, aligned place-value labels and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal build, exchange or jump steps separately; preserve correct work and reduce choices when useful.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed steps can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: repair a calm invention workshop using one checked calculation
gamification.reward: one blueprint spark for a model, strategy or check
gamification.retry_message: Your correct blocks and steps stay. Choose another model or clue and continue.

hints: Identify the start, change or compared quantities.
Check with an inverse, estimate or second representation.

feedback.correct: The calculation and model agree. 51 − 15 = 36; then 36 + 10 = 46.
feedback.repair: Rebuild the starting whole, mark the backward direction, remove tens and ones by place value, then check by adding the removed amount back.
feedback.strategy_message: Building, jumping, partitioning, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid evidence; speed and handwriting are not scored.

body.narration_script: Start with 52 shells, use 8, then receive 7. How many now?

body.number_line_route: Static or stepwise labelled jumps of tens and ones; movement animation is optional.
body.part_whole_route: A labelled whole-and-parts bar can replace dense text or block manipulation.
body.visual_route: One low-clutter representation per panel with large numerals, aligned place-value labels and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal build, exchange or jump steps separately; preserve correct work and reduce choices when useful.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed steps can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

Check with an inverse, estimate or second representation.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds all digits as separate ones without preserving tens and ones.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner gives answers such as 412 for 34 + 18.
repair_sequence: Separate tens and ones into labelled columns.
Count tens as groups, not loose ones.
Rebuild the calculation with rods and cubes.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner combines tens and ones correctly in a new calculation.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Ignores regrouping when ones cross ten.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner adds ones to 12 but keeps only the final digit or gives an impossible two-digit structure.
repair_sequence: Count ten ones into a full group.
Swap ten cubes for one ten-rod.
Name the new tens and remaining ones.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner completes a regrouping addition with the swap.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses addition when a subtraction comparison is needed.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects the sum for a take-away or difference problem.
repair_sequence: Highlight action words and object movement.
Ask learner to select add or subtract before solving.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects the correct operation in a new picture problem.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm workshop lighting and avoid fast spinning gear effects.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace regrouping animations with step-by-step static frames.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read calculations and model changes aloud with optional slower narration.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use number cards, rods and cubes before written word problems.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Chunk tasks into build, swap, answer and explain stages.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow learners to undo block moves and replay the regrouping swap.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use neutral repair prompts such as 'Let's rebuild the tens and ones'.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach add, subtract, total, left, tens, ones and swap with picture anchors.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate two-digit addition and subtraction with models; secure requires strategy choice and regrouping retention.
evidence.parent_summary: Can use tens and ones to add and subtract two-digit numbers, including some regrouping.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Builds two-digit calculations with base-ten models, regroups ones into tens and explains chosen strategies.
next_actions: Interleave addition and subtraction once operation choice is reliable.
Move from concrete blocks to number-line and mental strategies.
Use daily retrieval for decade-crossing calculations.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 two-digit addition and subtraction pack with a deterministic 240-variant pilot bank. Four curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks cover place-value partitioning; adding and subtracting ones, tens and two-digit numbers; concrete exchange across ten; efficient mental and written representations; inverse fact families; missing-number equations; comparison and one/two-step contexts; estimation, reasonableness, misconception diagnosis and spaced transfer. Every generated task includes base-ten, number-line and part-whole routes, reduced-load SEND/dyscalculia supports, alternative inputs, rich corrective feedback and pressure-free exploration without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected narrated contexts reference ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
